skill.name: quality-report-generate
skill.description: Generate comprehensive quality report with metrics and verification. Produces final CHAPTER_XX_VERIFICATION.md and quality_metrics.json for deployment approval.
skill.summary: Generate comprehensive quality report with metrics and verification. Produces final CHAPTER_XX_VERIF...
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many markdown backticks and sample file paths, but most are false positives from documentation and fenced examples. The skill does instruct the assistant to run a local Python reporting script and write quality artifacts, so it carries medium operational risk but no evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Local Script Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to change into a tools directory and run python3 generate_quality_report.py. This is a legitimate reporting workflow, but marketplace users should review the local script before allowing execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly documented and has a clear quality-report purpose. Risk remains because it executes local project code, but there is no evidence of obfuscation, persistence, network access, or credential use.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Writes to Chapter Artifacts
medium_findings.description: The skill writes markdown and JSON output files under output/chapter_XX/chapter_artifacts. This is expected behavior for the skill, but it can overwrite report artifacts if pointed at an existing chapter directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file paths are clearly documented as report outputs and command arguments. The risk is operational rather than malicious because writes are constrained to documented artifact paths.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: Most backtick execution and weak cryptography alerts are caused by inline markdown, fenced examples, .md filenames, and report samples. No evidence found of embedded Ruby execution or weak cryptographic code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation examples or fenced data blocks. They are not executable code in the skill package and do not define cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: Relative Path Traversal False Positive
low_findings.description: The ../ path segments appear in example command arguments that reference neighboring output artifacts from the Calypso tools directory. No evidence found that user-controlled paths are expanded outside the intended project workspace by the skill text itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are fixed examples for local chapter artifacts. A malicious traversal payload is not shown, though users should still confirm the invoked script validates inputs.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a bash example that runs a Python report generator with local file arguments. This pattern is acceptable for the skill purpose when the referenced script is trusted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is present in a fenced bash block and is central to the workflow. The pattern is dangerous in general because it executes local code, but context shows a legitimate report-generation intent.

content.value_statement: Chapter validation often leaves results scattered across multiple files. This skill combines validation outputs into clear reports, metrics, and deployment recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Aggregates structure, semantic, and consolidation validation artifacts.
Calculates overall quality scores and compliance percentages.
Produces a human-readable chapter verification report.
Produces machine-readable quality metrics for pipelines.
Documents deployment decisions, findings, and next steps.
Summarizes content counts, CSS classes, and validation status.
limitations: Requires existing validation artifact files from earlier gates.
Assumes the Calypso project output directory structure.
Does not independently verify visual accuracy beyond available inputs.
May overwrite existing report files in the target artifact directory.

use_cases.title: Approve Generated Chapters
use_cases.description: Create final verification evidence before publishing a generated HTML chapter.

use_cases.title: Track Quality Metrics
use_cases.description: Export structured metrics that can feed quality dashboards and CI checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare Review Packets
use_cases.description: Summarize validation results, findings, and recommendations for stakeholder review.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Basic Report
prompt_templates.scenario: You have completed validation for one chapter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the quality report for chapter 2 using the available validation artifacts and final HTML file.

prompt_templates.title: Review Deployment Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a publish or review decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a quality report for chapter 2 and highlight whether it is ready for deployment or needs manual review.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize Pipeline Metrics
prompt_templates.scenario: You need machine-readable results for automation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the markdown verification report and quality metrics JSON for chapter 2, then summarize the key CI metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Multiple Validation Inputs
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a detailed review across validation gates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the structure, semantic, and consolidation artifacts for chapter 2 to generate a complete quality report with risks, scores, and recommended fixes.

output_examples.input: Chapter 2 has passed structure and semantic validation with two minor warnings.
output_examples.output: A verification report states the chapter is ready for deployment, lists the two warnings, and records the overall quality score.

output_examples.input: Validation artifacts show missing semantic classes and incomplete consolidation data.
output_examples.output: The report marks the chapter as requiring review, explains the failed checks, and recommends specific fixes before deployment.

output_examples.input: A team needs dashboard data for generated chapter quality.
output_examples.output: The skill creates quality metrics with scores, status, content counts, and deployment readiness fields for pipeline ingestion.

best_practices: Run earlier validation gates before generating the final report.
Review the target output directory before writing report artifacts.
Compare the markdown report and metrics file before deployment approval.
anti_patterns: Do not use the report as the only validation step.
Do not run unreviewed local scripts from an untrusted repository.
Do not ignore warning counts when the report recommends manual review.

faq.question: What files does this skill expect?
faq.answer: It expects structure validation, semantic validation, consolidation metadata, and the final chapter HTML file.

faq.question: What does the skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a markdown verification report and a JSON metrics file for the chapter.

faq.question: Can it approve deployment automatically?
faq.answer: It can provide a deployment recommendation based on validation results, but teams should review important releases.

faq.question: Does it validate visual accuracy by itself?
faq.answer: No. It records visual accuracy results when those results are available from prior checks.

faq.question: Is it safe to run in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, when used in a trusted workspace with reviewed local scripts and expected chapter artifacts.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The workflow can run a local Python script and write files, so users should confirm the workspace is trusted.
